trade.summary: The US is among Spain's most important trading partners outside the EU. Spain exports vehicles, food products, pharmaceuticals, and machinery; the US exports aircraft, technology, pharmaceuticals, and industrial machinery. Spain has a modest trade surplus with the US that has occasionally attracted US attention under protectionist administrations.
disputes: US tariff threats on EU automotive and food exports (2025)

diplomatic.status: Close NATO allies with some value tensions
diplomatic.summary: Spain and the US are NATO allies with a generally positive relationship. The US maintains key military installations in Spain (Rota naval base, Moron de la Frontera air base). Under Trump 2.0, Spain-US ties face pressure over NATO defense spending demands, trade tariffs, and differences on Ukraine policy. Spain's left-wing government has been somewhat more critical of US positions than some other EU allies.
recent_incidents: US pressure on NATO defense spending targets (2025)

military.summary: The US maintains Rota naval base (key NATO asset with US destroyers deployed for Ballistic Missile Defense) and Moron de la Frontera air base in Spain. Spain is part of the US-led NATO command structure and contributes to NATO missions. Spain cooperates on Ukraine military support within NATO. US-Spain military cooperation is deep and institutionalized.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. US firms (Google, Microsoft, Amazon) have major Spain presences including data centers. Spanish firms (Santander, BBVA, Iberdrola, ACS, Ferrovial) have significant US operations — Ferrovial manages Chicago's O'Hare airport, Iberdrola is a major US renewable energy player. Financial ties are deep and growing.

energy.summary: The US has become a significant LNG supplier to Spain since 2022 as Spain diversified from Russian energy. US LNG exports to Spain have grown substantially. Spain is developing green hydrogen exports where it could compete with or complement US hydrogen ambitions.

scientific.summary: Very strong scientific partnership. Spanish and US researchers collaborate extensively across all disciplines. Many Spanish researchers work at US universities; American Fulbright scholars study in Spain. Joint research in astrophysics (Canary Islands observatories with US partnerships), climate science, and biomedical research is particularly strong.
